annotations.label: Continuous function (informal)

anchor.snippet: a continuous function is a function such that a small variation of the argument induces a small variation of the value of the function

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Continuous` formalizes this informal idea in the topological-space setting.

annotations.label: Discontinuous function

anchor.snippet: A discontinuous function is a function that is not continuous

annotations.note: Mathlib has no standalone `Discontinuous` predicate; one uses `¬ Continuous f` directly.

annotations.label: Height of growing flower (continuous)

anchor.snippet: the function H ( t ) denoting the height of a growing flower at time t would be considered continuous

annotations.note: This is an informal real-world illustration with no formal counterpart in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bank account (discontinuous)

anchor.snippet: the function M ( t ) denoting the amount of money in a bank account at time t would be considered discontinuous

annotations.note: Informal motivational example, not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Continuity at a real number via limits

anchor.snippet: A function f with variable x is continuous at the real number c , if the limit of

annotations.note: `ContinuousAt f x := Tendsto f (𝓝 x) (𝓝 (f x))` generalizes the real-valued limit definition.

annotations.label: Continuous on open interval

anchor.snippet: A function is continuous on an open interval if the interval is contained in the function's domain and the function is continuous at every interval point

annotations.note: `ContinuousOn f s` captures continuity on any subset, including open intervals.

annotations.label: Continuous on semi-open or closed interval

anchor.snippet: A function is continuous on a semi-open or a closed interval

annotations.note: `ContinuousOn f (Icc a b)` etc. covers continuity on closed and semi-open intervals.

annotations.label: Discontinuity of a partial function

anchor.snippet: A partial function is discontinuous at a point if the point belongs to the topological closure of its domain

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize discontinuity for partial functions as a named predicate.

annotations.label: Polynomial functions continuous everywhere

anchor.snippet: all polynomial functions are continuous everywhere

annotations.note: `Polynomial.continuous` states `Continuous (fun x => p.eval x)`.

annotations.label: Continuity at a point via limits

anchor.section: Definition in terms of limits of functions
anchor.snippet: The function f is continuous at some point c of its domain if the limit of

annotations.note: `ContinuousAt f x` is defined as `Tendsto f (𝓝 x) (𝓝 (f x))`, the filter version of limits.

anchors.snippet: The function f is continuous at some point c of its domain if the limit of

annotations.label: Continuity at a point via neighborhoods

anchor.snippet: a function f is continuous at a point c of its domain if, for any neighborhood

annotations.note: `ContinuousAt` is literally the neighborhood-filter characterization.

annotations.label: Real-valued function on integers is continuous

anchor.snippet: every real-valued function on the integers is continuous

annotations.note: `continuous_of_discreteTopology` (with `ℤ` having the discrete topology) gives this as a special case.

anchor.section: Definition in terms of limits of sequences
anchor.snippet: One can instead require that for any sequence

annotations.note: `SeqContinuous f` requires that `f` preserves limits of sequences.

annotations.note: Mathlib's metric-space `Metric.continuous_iff` and `continuousAt` variants give the ε–δ form.

annotations.label: Continuity via oscillation

anchor.snippet: a function f is continuous at a point

annotations.note: `oscillation_eq_zero` characterizes continuity at a point by vanishing oscillation.

annotations.label: Hyperreal/nonstandard continuity

anchor.snippet: In nonstandard analysis, continuity can be defined as follows

annotations.note: Mathlib does not develop nonstandard-analysis continuity in terms of hyperreals.

annotations.label: Rules for continuity

anchor.snippet: most functions are built from simpler functions, and their continuity can be deduced immediately from the way they are defined

annotations.note: Mathlib has extensive infrastructure (`Continuous.comp`, `Continuous.add`, `fun_prop` tactic, etc.) for combining continuous functions.

annotations.label: Polynomials and rational functions continuous

anchor.snippet: These rules imply that every polynomial function is continuous everywhere

annotations.note: `Polynomial.continuous` is formalized; continuity of rational functions on their domain is available via field operations but not as a single named statement.

annotations.label: Sinc function continuous

anchor.snippet: An example of a function for which the above rules are not sufficient is the sinc function

annotations.note: No `sinc` function appears to be defined in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: An example of a function for which the above rules are not sufficient is the sinc function

annotations.label: Heaviside step function

anchor.snippet: An example of a discontinuous function is the Heaviside step function

annotations.note: No Heaviside step function is defined in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: An example of a discontinuous function is the Heaviside step function

annotations.label: Signum function discontinuous at 0

anchor.snippet: the signum or sign function

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `SignType.sign`; discontinuity at 0 isn't stated as a named lemma.

anchors.snippet: the signum or sign function

annotations.label: Thomae's function

anchor.snippet: Thomae's function

annotations.note: Thomae's function is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dirichlet's function nowhere continuous

anchor.snippet: Dirichlet's function , the indicator function for the set of rational numbers

annotations.note: Dirichlet's indicator function is not formalized as a named example in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: Dirichlet's function , the indicator function for the set of rational numbers

anchors.value: {\displaystyle f(x)={\begin{cases}1&{\text{ if }}x=0\\{\frac {1}{q}}&{\text{ if }}x={\frac {p}{q}}{\text{(in lowest terms) is a rational number}}\\0&{\text{ if }}x{\text{ is irrational}}.\end{cases}}}
{\displaystyle D(x)={\begin{cases}0&{\text{ if }}x{\text{  is irrational }}(\in \mathbb {R} \setminus \mathbb {Q} )\\1&{\text{ if }}x{\text{ is rational }}(\in \mathbb {Q} )\end{cases}}}

annotations.label: Intermediate value theorem

anchor.snippet: If the real-valued function f is continuous on the closed interval

annotations.note: `intermediate_value_Icc` formalizes the classical IVT on a closed interval.

annotations.label: Child's height (IVT illustration)

anchor.snippet: if a child grows from 1 m to 1.5 m between the ages of two and six years

annotations.note: Informal narrative illustration of IVT, not formalized.

annotations.label: Extreme value theorem

anchor.snippet: The extreme value theorem states that if a function f is defined on a closed interval

annotations.note: `IsCompact.exists_isMinOn`/`exists_isMaxOn` give the EVT, generalized to compact sets.

annotations.label: Differentiable implies continuous

anchor.snippet: Every differentiable function

annotations.note: `Differentiable.continuous` is the standard statement.

anchors.snippet: Every differentiable function

annotations.label: Absolute value continuous but not differentiable at 0

anchor.snippet: the absolute value function

annotations.note: `continuous_abs` is in Mathlib; non-differentiability at 0 isn't stated as a single named result.

anchors.snippet: the absolute value function

annotations.label: Weierstrass function

anchor.snippet: Weierstrass's function is also everywhere continuous but nowhere differentiable

annotations.note: Weierstrass's nowhere-differentiable function is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Continuously differentiable / C^k class

anchor.snippet: If f′ ( x ) is continuous, f ( x ) is said to be continuously differentiable

annotations.note: `ContDiff n f` formalizes the C^n class of functions.

anchors.snippet: If f′ ( x ) is continuous, f ( x ) is said to be continuously differentiable

annotations.label: Continuous functions are integrable

anchor.snippet: Every continuous function

annotations.note: `ContinuousOn.intervalIntegrable` gives integrability of continuous functions on intervals.

anchors.snippet: Every continuous function

annotations.label: Pointwise limit of functions

anchor.snippet: the resulting function

annotations.note: Pointwise convergence is characterized by `tendsto_pi_nhds` on product topology.

anchors.snippet: the resulting function

annotations.label: Uniform convergence theorem

anchor.snippet: f is continuous if all functions

annotations.note: `TendstoUniformly.continuous` formalizes that uniform limits of continuous functions are continuous.

anchors.snippet: f is continuous if all functions

annotations.label: Right-continuous function

anchor.snippet: f is said to be right-continuous at the point c if the following holds

annotations.note: Right-continuity is expressed as `ContinuousWithinAt f (Ici c) c`, with `continuousWithinAt_Ioi_iff_Ici` available.

anchors.snippet: f is said to be right-continuous at the point c if the following holds

annotations.label: Continuous iff right- and left-continuous

anchor.snippet: A function is continuous if and only if it is both right-continuous and left-continuous

annotations.note: `continuousAt_iff_continuous_left_right` gives the equivalence.

annotations.label: Lower semi-continuity

anchor.snippet: A function f is lower semi-continuous at the point c if, roughly, any jumps that might occur only go down

annotations.note: `LowerSemicontinuous` and its `At`/`On`/`WithinAt` variants are in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: A function f is lower semi-continuous at the point c if, roughly, any jumps that might occur only go down

annotations.label: Continuity between metric spaces

anchor.snippet: is continuous at the point

annotations.note: `Metric.continuous_iff` states the ε–δ characterization between metric spaces.

anchors.snippet: is continuous at the point

annotations.label: Continuity points form a G-delta set

anchor.snippet: The set of points at which a function between metric spaces is continuous is a

annotations.note: Mathlib has the oscillation infrastructure to express this, but I did not find a direct named lemma asserting the continuity set is `IsGδ`.

annotations.label: Linear operator continuous iff bounded

anchor.snippet: a linear operator

annotations.note: `IsBoundedLinearMap` and `ContinuousLinearMap` plus `LinearMap.bound_of_continuous`/`mkContinuousOfExistsBound` formalize the bounded↔continuous equivalence for linear maps on normed spaces.

anchors.snippet: a linear operator

annotations.label: Uniform continuity

anchor.snippet: a function f as above is uniformly continuous if the

annotations.note: `UniformContinuous` is the standard Mathlib definition.

annotations.label: Uniformly continuous implies continuous

anchor.snippet: Thus, any uniformly continuous function is continuous

annotations.note: `UniformContinuous.continuous` is the standard implication.

annotations.label: Hölder continuity

anchor.snippet: A function is Hölder continuous with exponent α

annotations.note: `HolderWith C r f` gives the standard Hölder condition.

anchors.snippet: A function is Hölder continuous with exponent α

annotations.label: Lipschitz continuity

anchor.snippet: a function is Lipschitz continuous if there is a constant K

annotations.note: `LipschitzWith K f` is the standard Mathlib definition.

anchors.snippet: a function is Lipschitz continuous if there is a constant K

annotations.label: Continuity between topological spaces

anchor.snippet: between two topological spaces X and Y is continuous if for every open set

annotations.note: `Continuous` is defined by preimage of opens being open.

anchors.snippet: between two topological spaces X and Y is continuous if for every open set

annotations.label: Equivalent via closed preimages

anchor.snippet: This is equivalent to the condition that the preimages of the closed sets

annotations.note: `continuous_iff_isClosed` gives the closed-preimage characterization.

annotations.label: Discrete topology makes all maps continuous

anchor.snippet: if a set X is given the discrete topology

annotations.note: `continuous_of_discreteTopology` covers exactly this fact.

anchors.snippet: if a set X is given the discrete topology

annotations.label: Continuity at a point (topological)

anchor.snippet: is continuous at a point

annotations.note: `ContinuousAt f x` is the standard pointwise continuity definition.

annotations.label: Continuous iff continuous at every point

anchor.snippet: a function

annotations.note: `continuous_iff_continuousAt` states the equivalence.

annotations.label: Filter characterization of continuity at a point

anchor.snippet: is continuous at

annotations.note: `ContinuousAt f x := Tendsto f (𝓝 x) (𝓝 (f x))` is exactly the neighborhood-filter form.

annotations.label: Sequentially continuous function

anchor.snippet: is sequentially continuous if whenever a sequence

annotations.note: `SeqContinuous` is the standard definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Theorem: continuity iff sequential continuity (real case)
annotations.note: `continuous_iff_seqContinuous` holds in any sequential space; reals are sequential.

annotations.label: Theorem: equivalent forms via closure/interior
annotations.note: `continuous_iff_image_closure_subset_closure_image`, together with `continuous_iff_isClosed`/`continuous_iff_isOpen`, gives the equivalent forms.

annotations.label: Continuity via filters

anchor.snippet: Continuity can also be characterized in terms of filters

annotations.note: Mathlib defines continuity via filter `Tendsto` and has filter/ultrafilter characterizations.

annotations.label: Composition of continuous functions

anchor.snippet: are continuous, then so is the composition

annotations.note: `Continuous.comp` is the standard composition lemma.

annotations.label: Continuous image of compact is compact

anchor.snippet: X is compact , then f ( X ) is compact

annotations.note: `IsCompact.image` formalizes this property.

annotations.label: Continuous image of connected is connected

anchor.snippet: X is connected , then f ( X ) is connected

annotations.note: `IsConnected.image` (and `IsPreconnected.image`) formalize this.

annotations.label: Continuous image of path-connected is path-connected

anchor.snippet: X is path-connected , then f ( X ) is path-connected

annotations.note: `IsPathConnected.image` formalizes this.

annotations.label: Continuous image of Lindelöf is Lindelöf

anchor.snippet: X is Lindelöf , then f ( X ) is Lindelöf

annotations.note: `IsLindelof.image` formalizes this property.

annotations.label: Continuous image of separable is separable

anchor.snippet: X is separable , then f ( X ) is separable

annotations.note: `IsSeparable.image` formalizes this property.

annotations.label: Identity continuous iff coarser

anchor.snippet: the identity map

annotations.note: `continuous_id_iff_le` gives the equivalence with the order on topologies.

annotations.label: Open map

anchor.snippet: Symmetric to the concept of a continuous map is an open map , for which images of open sets are open

annotations.note: `IsOpenMap f := ∀ U, IsOpen U → IsOpen (f '' U)`.

annotations.label: Homeomorphism

anchor.snippet: A bijective continuous function with a continuous inverse function is called a homeomorphism

annotations.note: `Homeomorph X Y` (notation `X ≃ₜ Y`) is the standard structure.

annotations.label: Continuous bijection from compact to Hausdorff is homeomorphism

anchor.snippet: If a continuous bijection has as its domain a compact space and its codomain is Hausdorff

annotations.note: `Continuous.homeoOfEquivCompactToT2` directly gives this fact.

annotations.label: Final topology

anchor.snippet: the final topology on S is defined by letting the open sets of S be those subsets A of S

annotations.note: `TopologicalSpace.coinduced` is the single-map version; `iSup` over a family yields the general final topology.

anchors.snippet: the final topology on S is defined by letting the open sets of S be those subsets A of S

annotations.label: Initial topology

anchor.snippet: the initial topology on S is defined by designating as an open set every subset A of S

annotations.note: `TopologicalSpace.induced` (with `iInf` over a family) realizes the initial topology.

annotations.label: Topology determined by continuous functions

anchor.snippet: A topology on a set S is uniquely determined by the class of all continuous functions

annotations.note: Mathlib has the order-theoretic framework (`continuous_id_iff_le`, induced/coinduced Galois connection) implying this, but it's not stated as a single uniqueness theorem.

annotations.label: Continuous extension

anchor.snippet: a continuous extension of

annotations.note: `IsDenseInducing.extend` constructs the continuous extension along a dense-inducing map.

annotations.label: Uniqueness of continuous extension on dense subset

anchor.snippet: a continuous extension of

annotations.note: `Continuous.ext_on` gives uniqueness of a continuous extension on a dense set into a Hausdorff space.

annotations.label: Blumberg theorem

anchor.snippet: The Blumberg theorem states that if

annotations.note: No instance of Blumberg's theorem is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Scott-continuous (order-theoretic continuity)

anchor.snippet: an order-preserving function

annotations.note: `ScottContinuousOn` (and `ScottContinuous`) capture order-theoretic Scott continuity.

annotations.label: Continuous functor

anchor.snippet: a functor

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes continuous functors as functors preserving (small) limits via `PreservesLimits`, but I did not find a single decl named `ContinuousFunctor`.

anchors.snippet: a functor

annotations.label: Continuity space

anchor.snippet: A continuity space is a generalization of metric spaces and posets

annotations.note: Continuity spaces (Flagg) are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Approximately continuous function

anchor.snippet: is called approximately continuous at a point

annotations.note: No `ApproximatelyContinuous` notion appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stepanov–Denjoy theorem

anchor.snippet: A fundamental result known as the Stepanov-Denjoy theorem states that a function is measurable if and only if it is approximately continuous almost everywhere

annotations.note: The Stepanov–Denjoy theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.
